class.name: ResonanceAmbisonicPlayer
class.brief_description: Ambisonics (HOA) playback through Steam Audio: optional rotation, SN3D conversion, HRTF decode, and output leveling.
class.description: Assign a [ResonanceAmbisonicInternalStream] to [member stream] (other types log a warning). Channel layout and order come from the stream's [member ResonanceAmbisonicInternalStream.ambisonic_order] and [member ResonanceAmbisonicInternalStream.channel_streams]. Listener orientation comes from the active [Camera3D] when available. When [member rotation_enabled] is on and an HOA bed orientation is resolved ([member use_bed_scene_orientation], optional [member ambisonic_orientation_node], or the first [Node3D] parent), the decoder orientation is derived from the bed's world transform and the listener's world-to-local rotation so the sound field can follow that node while head-tracking still comes from the camera.

members.member: If [code]true[/code], decodes relative to listener head-tracking. When a scene HOA bed orientation is available (see [member use_bed_scene_orientation]), the pipeline combines the bed's transform with the listener before Ambisonics decode instead of leaving the bed fixed in world axes. If [code]false[/code], HOA is treated as already world-aligned: decode orientation uses the listener pose only for panning/HRTF—similar to authoring the bed in fixed world axes.

When [code]true[/code] with [member rotation_enabled], the HOA bed inherits orientation from [member ambisonic_orientation_node] if set; otherwise from the nearest [Node3D] ancestor, so the Ambisonic frame moves with that spatial branch. Set to [code]false[/code] to keep a world-fixed bed even when nested under moving nodes—useful for authored world-coordinate clips.
Optional [Node3D] defining the HOA bed basis (typically the object the sound field follows). Leave empty to use the first [Node3D] found when walking up from this player toward the scene root.

Use Steam Audio HRTF for binaural stereo decoding when an HRTF is loaded on [ResonanceServer]. When off or when no HRTF is available, decoding uses loudspeaker panning (lower CPU, less externalization). Matches the intent of common engines' [i]Apply HRTF[/i] toggle on Ambisonic listeners.

If [code]true[/code], Ambisonic channel samples are treated as [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ambisonic_data_exchange_formats]SN3D[/url]-normalized (common for third-party Ambisonic assets); internally converted to N3D before rotation/decode. Set to [code]false[/code] only if your [member stream] channels are already N3D (or you know your toolchain outputs N3D directly).

If [code]true[/code], applies the usual Ambisonic decoder nominal scaling ([code]1/sqrt(4*pi)[/code]) after decode so a constant omnidirectional (W-only) input has comparable peak level to an unspatialized mono clip. Disable if you normalize or gain-stage elsewhere.
